tagline: Calculate your Body Mass Index and health category
keywords: BMI, body mass index, weight category, health screening
calcFn: var totalIn = heightFt * 12 + heightIn; var bmi = (weight / (totalIn * totalIn)) * 703; var category = bmi < 18.5 ? 'Underweight' : bmi < 25 ? 'Normal weight' : bmi < 30 ? 'Overweight' : 'Obese'; return {bmi: fmt(bmi,1), category: category};

faq.a: 18.5-24.9 is considered normal weight.
It is a screening tool. Athletes with high muscle mass may show elevated BMI.

formulas.name: BMI Formula
formulas.explanation: Weight in pounds, height in inches.

tagline: Calculate your calorie deficit for weight loss goals
keywords: calorie deficit, weight loss, TDEE, diet, calories
calcFn: var mult = {'Sedentary': 13, 'Lightly Active': 14.5, 'Moderately Active': 16, 'Very Active': 18}; var tdee = weight * (mult[activity] || 14.5); var deficitCal = goalLbs * 500; var target = Math.max(tdee - deficitCal, 1200); var weeks = 20 / goalLbs; return {tdee: fmt(tdee,0), target: fmt(target,0), weeks: fmt(weeks,0)};

faq.q: How much deficit for weight loss?
faq.a: 500 cal/day deficit = ~1 lb/week. 1000 cal/day = ~2 lbs/week.
Generally 1200 for women, 1500 for men minimum to meet nutritional needs.

formulas.name: Deficit
formulas.explanation: Subtract 500 calories per pound you want to lose per week.

tagline: Estimate your take-home pay after taxes
keywords: paycheck, take home pay, tax withholding, net pay
calcFn: var periods = {'Weekly': 52, 'Bi-Weekly': 26, 'Monthly': 12}; var annual = gross * (periods[frequency] || 26); var fedRate = annual > 95375 ? 0.22 : annual > 44725 ? 0.17 : 0.12; var federal = gross * fedRate; var fica = gross * 0.0765; var state = gross * (stateTax/100); var net = gross - federal - fica - state; return {federal: dollar(federal), fica: dollar(fica), state: dollar(state), net: dollar(net)};

faq.a: Typically 25-35% of gross pay goes to federal, state, and FICA taxes.
Social Security (6.2%) and Medicare (1.45%) = 7.65% total.

formulas.name: Net Pay
formulas.explanation: Gross pay minus all tax withholdings.

tagline: Compare the cost of renting versus buying a home
keywords: rent vs buy, mortgage, renting, home buying decision
calcFn: var loan = homePrice * 0.8; var r = rate/100/12; var mortgage = loan * r / (1 - Math.pow(1+r,-360)); var monthlyOwn = mortgage + homePrice*0.01/12; var totalRent = rent * years * 12; var totalOwn = monthlyOwn * years * 12 + homePrice*0.2; var equity = homePrice*0.2 + homePrice*0.03*years; var netOwn = totalOwn - equity; var verdict = netOwn < totalRent ? 'Buying saves ' + dollar(totalRent-netOwn) : 'Renting saves ' + dollar(netOwn-totalRent); return {monthlyMortgage: dollar(mortgage), totalRent: dollar(totalRent), totalOwn: dollar(netOwn), verdict: verdict};

faq.a: Buy if staying 5+ years and can afford 20% down. Rent if you need flexibility.
Mortgage, property tax, insurance, maintenance, and closing costs.

tagline: Estimate the cost of your upcoming move
keywords: moving cost, movers, relocation, moving estimate
calcFn: var baseCost = {'DIY Truck': 200, 'Labor Only': 600, 'Full Service': 1500}; var base = baseCost[type] || 600; var sizeMult = bedrooms * 0.5 + 0.5; var distMult = distance > 100 ? 1 + distance/500 : 1; var estimate = base * sizeMult * distMult; var timeline = distance > 500 ? '2-4 days' : 'Same day'; return {estimate: dollar(estimate), timeline: timeline};

faq.a: Local: $500-2500. Long distance: $2000-8000+ depending on size.
Move mid-month/mid-week, declutter before, and get 3+ quotes.

formulas.name: Cost
formulas.explanation: Base rate scaled for home size and moving distance.

tagline: Calculate mulch needed for garden beds and landscaping
keywords: mulch, garden mulch, landscaping, mulch calculator
calcFn: var cuFt = area * (depth/12); var cubicYards = cuFt / 27; var bags = Math.ceil(cuFt / 2); var cost = cubicYards * 35; return {cubicYards: fmt(cubicYards,1), bags: fmt(bags,0), cost: dollar(cost)};

faq.a: 2-3 inches for most beds. Too deep can suffocate plant roots.
Bulk is cheaper for areas over 100 sq ft.

formulas.name: Volume
formulas.explanation: Area times depth converted to cubic yards.

keywords: one rep max, 1RM, strength, weightlifting, PR
calcFn: var oneRM = Math.round(weight * (1 + reps/30)); var pct90 = Math.round(oneRM * 0.9); var pct80 = Math.round(oneRM * 0.8); var pct70 = Math.round(oneRM * 0.7); return {oneRM: fmt(oneRM,0), pct90: fmt(pct90,0), pct80: fmt(pct80,0), pct70: fmt(pct70,0)};

faq.a: The maximum weight you can lift for a single repetition.
Most accurate with 3-5 rep sets. Less accurate above 10 reps.

tagline: Calculate what it costs to run any appliance
keywords: electricity cost, appliance cost, power bill, kWh cost
calcFn: var kwh = watts * hoursDay / 1000; var daily = kwh * rate; var monthly = daily * 30; var annual = daily * 365; return {daily: dollar(daily), monthly: dollar(monthly), annual: dollar(annual)};

faq.a: HVAC, water heater, dryer, and oven are the biggest consumers.
Use LED bulbs, Energy Star appliances, and run heavy loads off-peak.

formulas.name: Cost
formulas.explanation: Watt-hours to kWh times your electric rate.

tagline: Calculate grout needed for your tile project
keywords: grout, tile grout, tiling, grout coverage
calcFn: var tileArea = tileWidth * tileLength; var jointLengthPerTile = 2 * (tileWidth + tileLength); var tilesNeeded = area * 144 / tileArea; var totalJointLength = tilesNeeded * jointLengthPerTile / 2; var groutVolume = totalJointLength * jointWidth * 0.375; var groutLbs = groutVolume / 144 * 100; var bags25 = Math.ceil(groutLbs / 25); return {groutLbs: fmt(Math.max(groutLbs,5),0), bags25: fmt(Math.max(bags25,1),0)};

faq.q: How much grout do I need?
faq.a: Depends on tile size and grout line width. Smaller tiles need more grout.
Sanded for joints 1/8 inch+, unsanded for smaller joints.

formulas.name: Grout
formulas.formula: Based on joint length × width × depth per tile
formulas.explanation: Total grout joint volume across all tiles.

tagline: Plan your debt-free journey with the snowball method
keywords: debt snowball, debt payoff, debt free, financial freedom
calcFn: var totalDebt = debt1 + debt2; var months1 = Math.ceil(debt1 / extra); var months2 = months1 + Math.ceil(debt2 / (extra + debt1*rate1/100/12)); var debtFreeMonths = months2; var minPayment1 = debt1 * 0.03; var minPayment2 = debt2 * 0.03; var minMonths = Math.ceil(totalDebt / (minPayment1 + minPayment2)); var interestSaved = totalDebt * 0.15 * ((minMonths - debtFreeMonths) / 12); return {totalDebt: dollar(totalDebt), debtFreeMonths: fmt(debtFreeMonths,0), interestSaved: dollar(Math.max(interestSaved,0))};

faq.a: Pay off smallest debt first for motivation wins, then roll payments to next debt.
Snowball targets smallest balance. Avalanche targets highest interest rate.

formulas.name: Snowball
formulas.formula: Pay minimum on all, throw extra at smallest debt
formulas.explanation: Freed-up payments roll into the next debt for accelerating payoff.

tagline: Calculate concrete mix materials for custom batches
keywords: concrete mix, cement, sand, gravel, concrete ratio
calcFn: var ratios = {'1:2:3 (standard)': [1,2,3], '1:1.5:3 (strong)': [1,1.5,3], '1:3:6 (lean)': [1,3,6]}; var r = ratios[mixRatio] || [1,2,3]; var total = r[0]+r[1]+r[2]; var cementCuFt = cubicFeet * r[0] / total * 1.54; var cement = Math.ceil(cementCuFt / 1); var sand = cubicFeet * r[1] / total * 1.54; var gravel = cubicFeet * r[2] / total * 1.54; return {cement: fmt(cement,0), sand: fmt(sand,0), gravel: fmt(gravel,0)};

faq.q: What is the standard concrete mix ratio?
faq.a: 1:2:3 (cement:sand:gravel) by volume for general purpose concrete.
1:1.5:3 for structural elements, foundations, and load-bearing applications.

formulas.name: Material Volumes
formulas.formula: Each = Total × Ratio Part / Sum × 1.54 bulking factor
formulas.explanation: Divide total by ratio parts with bulking factor for dry materials.

tagline: Calculate how much you need in your emergency fund
keywords: emergency fund, savings, rainy day, financial safety net
calcFn: var baseMonths = {'Very Stable': 3, 'Stable': 6, 'Variable': 9, 'Unstable': 12}; var months = (baseMonths[stability] || 6) + (dependents > 0 ? 1 : 0); var minimum = monthlyExpenses * 3; var recommended = monthlyExpenses * months; return {minimum: dollar(minimum), recommended: dollar(recommended), months: fmt(months,0)};

faq.a: 3-6 months expenses for stable income, 6-12 for variable income.
High-yield savings account for easy access and interest earnings.

tagline: Calculate area in square feet for rooms, yards, and projects
keywords: square footage, area calculator, room size, sq ft
calcFn: var sqft = length * width; var sqm = sqft * 0.0929; var acres = sqft / 43560; return {sqft: fmt(sqft,1), sqm: fmt(sqm,1), acres: fmt(acres,4)};

faq.q: How do I calculate square footage?
faq.a: Multiply length by width. For irregular shapes, break into rectangles.

faq.q: How many square feet in an acre?
faq.a: 43,560 square feet = 1 acre.

tagline: Calculate soil needed for garden beds and pots
keywords: soil, garden soil, topsoil, potting mix, fill dirt
calcFn: var cuFt = length * width * (depth/12); var cubicYards = cuFt / 27; var bags = Math.ceil(cuFt); var cost = cubicYards * 30; return {cubicYards: fmt(cubicYards,2), bags: fmt(bags,0), cost: dollar(cost)};

faq.q: How deep should garden soil be?
faq.a: 6 inches for shallow-root plants, 12+ inches for vegetables and deep roots.
Topsoil is natural, garden soil is amended with compost and nutrients.

formulas.name: Volume
formulas.explanation: Length times width times depth converted to cubic yards.

tagline: Calculate monthly payment for any loan
keywords: loan payment, monthly payment, loan calculator, amortization
calcFn: var r = rate/100/12; var payment = principal * r / (1 - Math.pow(1+r, -term)); var totalPaid = payment * term; var totalInterest = totalPaid - principal; return {payment: dollar(payment), totalInterest: dollar(totalInterest), totalPaid: dollar(totalPaid)};

faq.a: Using the amortization formula: PMT = P × r / (1-(1+r)^-n).

faq.q: Should I choose a shorter term?
faq.a: Shorter terms have higher payments but much less total interest.

formulas.name: Payment
formulas.explanation: Standard amortization formula for fixed-rate loans.

tagline: Find your federal tax bracket and estimated tax
keywords: tax bracket, income tax, federal tax, marginal rate
calcFn: var brackets; if (status === 'Single') brackets = [[11600,0.10],[47150,0.12],[100525,0.22],[191950,0.24],[243725,0.32],[609350,0.35],[Infinity,0.37]]; else if (status === 'Married Filing Jointly') brackets = [[23200,0.10],[94300,0.12],[201050,0.22],[383900,0.24],[487450,0.32],[731200,0.35],[Infinity,0.37]]; else brackets = [[16550,0.10],[63100,0.12],[100500,0.22],[191950,0.24],[243700,0.32],[609350,0.35],[Infinity,0.37]]; var tax = 0; var prev = 0; var bracket = 10; for (var i = 0; i < brackets.length; i++) { var top = brackets[i][0]; var rate = brackets[i][1]; if (income <= top) { tax += (income - prev) * rate; bracket = rate * 100; break; } tax += (top - prev) * rate; prev = top; } var effectiveRate = (tax / income) * 100; return {bracket: fmt(bracket,0), taxOwed: dollar(tax), effectiveRate: fmt(effectiveRate,1)};

faq.a: Your marginal rate — the rate on your last dollar of income. Effective rate is lower.
Marginal is your bracket rate. Effective is total tax / total income — always lower.

formulas.name: Progressive Tax
formulas.formula: Tax = Sum of (income in each bracket × bracket rate)
formulas.explanation: Each portion of income is taxed at its respective bracket rate.
